title: Storage and Persistent Volumes
description: Configure persistent storage solutions including PVs, PVCs, and StatefulSets
context: You need to set up persistent storage for applications that require data persistence across pod restarts.

tasks.title: Create PersistentVolume
tasks.description: Create a PersistentVolume named 'app-pv' with 2Gi capacity, ReadWriteOnce access mode, and hostPath /opt/data

commands: kubectl get pv app-pv --no-headers | grep Available

hints: Use hostPath storage class for local development
Set capacity to 2Gi and accessModes to ReadWriteOnce

tasks.title: Create PersistentVolumeClaim
tasks.description: Create a PVC named 'app-pvc' in namespace 'storage-test' that requests 1Gi storage with ReadWriteOnce access

commands: kubectl get pvc app-pvc -n storage-test --no-headers | grep Bound

hints: Create the namespace first if it doesn't exist
Don't specify storageClassName to bind to the PV

tasks.title: Create pod with volume mount
tasks.description: Create a pod named 'storage-pod' that uses the PVC and mounts it at /data

commands: kubectl get pod storage-pod -n storage-test --no-headers | grep Running

hints: Use volumes and volumeMounts in the pod spec
Mount the PVC at /data inside the container
Use a simple image like busybox or nginx

tasks.title: Test data persistence
tasks.description: Write data to the mounted volume and verify it persists after pod restart
hints: Use 'kubectl exec' to write a file to /data

resources.name: pv-template
resources.content:
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolume
metadata:
  name: app-pv
spec:
  capacity:
    storage: 2Gi
  accessModes:
    - ReadWriteOnce
  hostPath:
    path: /opt/data

resources.name: pvc-template
resources.content:
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: app-pvc
  namespace: storage-test
spec:
  accessModes:
    - ReadWriteOnce
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: 1Gi

solution: # Step 4: Create pod with volume mount
kubectl exec storage-pod -n storage-test -- sh -c 'echo "test data" > /data/test-file.txt'
